2076235616560284001.full_text:
The biggest question should be at this point, what is the biggest cause that India is suddenly looking this fragile?

A couple of months ago, we were unbeatable and experts claimed India had 3 backup teams. But if we look around, there isn’t even 1. Were they too reliant on Hardik, Bumrah, and the seniors, and now the cracks are open. What is exactly creating this issue? Whether it is selection, IPL pitches, or something else. This England series is horrible… our batters, bowlers, and spinners…all are clueless.

So what is exactly the place to fix?

2076225046943506612.full_text: Abhishek Nayar said : “The quality I admired most about Rohit was that he always took responsibility. He would never come out and blame anyone by saying, ‘You did this’ or ‘You did that.’ Instead, he would put his hand up and say, ‘I failed.’ You would never hear him making excuses or throwing someone under the bus.” [Jiostar]

2076250115967537233.full_text:
MUNAF PATEL EXPOSED INDIAN BATTERS ON OVERSEAS MATCHES 🥶

Munaf Patel said: "Where the ball swings or turns, you have seen the result. Why? Because you have started playing with a 'stick' (referring to non-swinging/fast bowlers). The one who bowls at 150 kph, the ball comes straight, it doesn't turn, it doesn't swing. You are hitting sixes with ease against them. In every team, there are three such 'stick' bowlers. If every team has three such bowlers, then when will you face swing bowling?"
